meaning: adj.十分简单的,十分安全的,极坚固的
examples: The treatment will be tried out on human beings only after it has been shown to be safe and foolproof in animals.
If you are considering a supplement, read the list of ingredients, she says, although this is not always foolproof.
The system is not 100 per cent foolproof.
There's no such thing as a foolproof trading indicator, but here's one that comes remarkably close.
Therein lies the most important lesson of all to any aspiring restaurateur: success is never foolproof.
